2604.16275.title: No Universal Courtesy: A Cross-Linguistic, Multi-Model Study of Politeness Effects on LLMs Using the PLUM Corpus
2604.16275.paper_summary: This paper explores the response of Large Language Models (LLMs) to user prompts with different degrees of politeness and impoliteness. The Politeness Theory by Brown and Levinson and the Impoliteness Framework by Culpeper form the basis of experiments conducted across three languages (English, Hindi, Spanish), five models (Gemini-Pro, GPT-4o Mini, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, DeepSeek-Chat, and Llama 3), and three interaction histories between users (raw, polite, and impolite). Our sample consists of 22,500 pairs of prompts and responses of various types, evaluated across five levels of politeness using an eight-factor assessment framework: coherence, clarity, depth, responsiveness, context retention, toxicity, conciseness, and readability. The findings show that model performance is highly influenced by tone, dialogue history, and language. While polite prompts enhance the average response quality by up to ~11% and impolite tones worsen it, these effects are neither consistent nor universal across languages and models. English is best served by courteous or direct tones, Hindi by deferential and indirect tones, and Spanish by assertive tones. Among the models, Llama is the most tone-sensitive (11.5% range), whereas GPT is more robust to adversarial tone. These results indicate that politeness is a quantifiable computational variable that affects LLM behaviour, though its impact is language- and model-dependent rather than universal. To support reproducibility and future work, we additionally release PLUM (Politeness Levels in Utterances, Multilingual), a publicly available corpus of 1,500 human-validated prompts across three languages and five politeness categories, and provide a formal supplementary analysis of six falsifiable hypotheses derived from politeness theory, empirically assessed against the dataset.
2604.16275.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文探討大型語言模型（LLMs）對不同程度的禮貌和不禮貌用戶提示的反應。布朗和萊文森的禮貌理論以及卡爾佩珀的不禮貌框架構成了在三種語言（英語、印地語、西班牙語）、五個模型（Gemini-Pro、GPT-4o Mini、Claude 3.7 Sonnet、DeepSeek-Chat 和 Llama 3）以及三種用戶互動歷史（原始、禮貌和不禮貌）中進行實驗的基礎。我們的樣本包含22,500對各種類型的提示和回應，並使用八個因素的評估框架在五個禮貌水平上進行評估：連貫性、清晰度、深度、反應性、上下文保留、毒性、簡潔性和可讀性。研究結果顯示，模型性能受到語氣、對話歷史和語言的高度影響。雖然禮貌的提示使平均回應質量提高了約11%，而不禮貌的語氣則使其惡化，但這些效果在不同語言和模型中並不一致或普遍。英語最適合使用禮貌或直接的語氣，印地語則適合使用恭敬和間接的語氣，而西班牙語則適合使用果斷的語氣。在這些模型中，Llama對語氣最敏感（範圍為11.5%），而GPT對對抗性語氣則更具穩健性。這些結果表明，禮貌是一個可量化的計算變量，影響LLM的行為，儘管其影響是依賴於語言和模型，而非普遍適用。為了支持可重複性和未來的研究，我們還發布了PLUM（語句中的禮貌水平，多語言），這是一個公開可用的語料庫，包含1,500個經人類驗證的提示，涵蓋三種語言和五個禮貌類別，並提供了基於禮貌理論衍生的六個可證偽假設的正式補充分析，這些假設經過實證評估與數據集進行比較。
2604.16275.author: Hitesh Mehta et.al.
2604.16275.authors: Hitesh Mehta, Arjit Saxena, Garima Chhikara, Rohit Kumar
